title: Refined Printer Cabinet
summary: A hardwood plywood cabinet sized for a printer, with a face frame, moulding detail, bun feet, and a single drawer below.
description:
This is a fairly plain plywood box elevated by a handful of finishing details: a proper applied face frame instead of raw plywood edges, moulding wrapped around the sides for visual interest, and turned bun feet in place of the box just sitting on the floor. Each of these is optional in the sense that the cabinet works without them, but together they're what separate a "refined" cabinet from a plain storage box — skip all of them and you still have a functional printer stand, just a plainer one.

The drawer below gets the same treatment as any Euro-slide drawer in this catalog: build it to your actual opening and your actual slide's clearance spec, not the plan's stated numbers, and consider attaching the handle before the drawer goes into the cabinet — much easier to drill a handle hole on a drawer face sitting flat on the bench than reaching into an installed drawer.

tools.note: For trimming edges to allow for edge banding, and for ripping moulding to width if needed.

cutList.note: Cut to fit your actual opening and slides.

steps.title: Attach the shelves
steps.body:
Drill 3/4" pocket holes facing upward on the insides of the sides for attaching the top later. Attach the shelves to the sides — the middle shelf can be fixed or left floating on shelf pins.

For a cleaner edge, consider trimming 1/4" off the front edge of each shelf and applying edge banding to it.

steps.title: Build and attach the face frame
steps.body: Build the face frame with 3/4" pocket holes, 1-1/4" pocket hole screws, and glue. Attach it to the front of the cabinet with 1-1/4" finish nails and glue.

steps.title: Add the drawer cleats
steps.body: Bring the sides in for the drawer slides by attaching 1x2 cleats to the insides with 1-1/4" finish nails and glue.

steps.title: Attach the top
steps.body: Attach the top through the pocket holes drilled earlier, with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Add the moulding and feet
steps.body: Add decorative moulding around the sides for detail. Attach the bun feet to the base.

steps.title: Build the drawer
steps.body: Measure the opening in the cabinet for the drawer. Check your drawer slides' clearance requirement (typically 1/2" per side, 1" total) and build the drawer box to fit — sides cut to the length of the slides, front and back cut to the desired width minus 1-1/2".

steps.title: Install the drawer and attach the face
steps.body:
Install the drawer box on its slides inside the cabinet. Place the drawer face over the box with an even gap on the sides and top, nail and glue in place, then add a few 1-1/4" screws from the inside for a secure hold.

It may be easier to attach the handle to the drawer face before installing the drawer in the cabinet.

steps.title: Attach the back and finish
steps.body: Attach the back panel, sand every surface, and finish.

images.alt: Refined printer cabinet with bun feet and a single drawer
